articles.title: LIVE: Trump urges allies to send warships to protect Strait of Hormuz
articles.description: Former U.S. president Donald Trump has called on allied nations to deploy naval forces to secure the Strait of Hormuz as tensions between Iran and Western allies intensify. The waterway carries nearly one-fifth of global oil supplies.

articles.title: LIVE: Iran missile strikes trigger regional security fears
articles.description: Missile launches linked to Iran have heightened tensions across the Gulf region with security concerns spreading to nearby states and international shipping routes.

articles.title: Ukraine fears losing Western attention as focus shifts to Middle East
articles.description: Ukrainian officials warn that global attention is moving away from the war with Russia as geopolitical focus shifts toward rising tensions in the Middle East.

articles.title: White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles diagnosed with early stage breast cancer
articles.description: The White House confirmed that Chief of Staff Susie Wiles has been diagnosed with early stage breast cancer. Officials say treatment plans are being reviewed and the condition was detected early.

articles.title: Tinubu moves aviation safety bureau to Presidency
articles.description: Nigeria’s President Bola Tinubu has ordered the relocation of the aviation safety investigation bureau to the Presidency, overruling Transport Minister Festus Keyamo.

articles.title: ADC releases timetable for nationwide congresses
articles.description: Nigeria’s African Democratic Congress has released a schedule for congresses across the country ahead of the party’s national convention.

articles.title: Wike-backed PDP faction vows to rebuild party in Ekiti
articles.description: A faction of the Peoples Democratic Party aligned with former governor Nyesom Wike says it is working to rebuild the party structure in Ekiti State.

articles.title: Negotiations intensify across French cities after mayoral vote
articles.description: Political negotiations are underway in several French cities following the first round of municipal elections as parties prepare alliances for the second round.

articles.title: What Gulf conflict could mean for Nigeria’s economy
articles.description: Economists say rising oil prices triggered by Middle East tensions could boost Nigeria’s revenue but also increase fuel and transportation costs.

articles.title: PalmPay expands offices nationwide to boost financial inclusion
articles.description: Digital finance company PalmPay says it is expanding operations across Nigeria in an effort to increase access to mobile payment services.

articles.title: Togo seeks additional electricity imports from Nigeria
articles.description: Authorities in Togo have opened discussions with Nigeria to increase electricity imports as energy demand rises across the region.

articles.title: Boko Haram and ISWAP increasingly target military bases
articles.description: Security analysts say extremist groups Boko Haram and Islamic State West Africa Province are focusing attacks on military installations to seize weapons and weaken regional security forces.

articles.title: Four die in Lagos-Ibadan expressway crash
articles.description: At least four people were killed and several others injured after a major accident along the Lagos-Ibadan Expressway, authorities confirmed.

articles.title: Police investigate extortion allegations involving Lagos task force
articles.description: Authorities have launched an investigation after viral videos appeared to show officers from the Lagos State Task Force demanding money from motorists.

articles.title: Mob attacks suspected robbery suspect in Enugu market
articles.description: A suspected robbery suspect was attacked by a crowd in a busy Enugu market before police intervened to restore order.

articles.title: Police arrest suspected burglar in Lagos
articles.description: Police say they have arrested a suspected burglar and recovered a firearm during an operation in Lagos.

articles.title: Two dead in meningitis outbreak linked to UK university
articles.description: Health authorities say two people have died and 11 others have been infected in a meningitis outbreak connected to a university in England.

articles.title: Scientists warn Nigerians about dangers of poor sleep
articles.description: Medical researchers say long-term sleep deprivation may increase the risk of heart disease, anxiety and weakened immune systems.

articles.title: Veteran gospel singer Toun Soetan dies at 73
articles.description: Nigeria’s gospel music community is mourning veteran singer Toun Soetan whose career spanned decades and influenced generations of artists.

articles.title: Asake seeks spiritual renewal during visit to Mecca
articles.description: Nigerian music star Asake has shared images of his visit to Mecca where he says he is focusing on spiritual reflection.

articles.title: Nathan Fillion planning animated reboot of Firefly
articles.description: Actor Nathan Fillion says he hopes to develop a new animated version of the cult science fiction series Firefly.

articles.title: Bahrain and Saudi Arabian Formula One races cancelled
articles.description: Formula One organisers have cancelled upcoming races in Bahrain and Saudi Arabia amid security concerns linked to tensions in the Middle East.

articles.title: Enyimba offers fans free entry for Plateau clash
articles.description: Nigerian football club Enyimba has announced free stadium entry for supporters attending the upcoming match against Plateau United.

articles.title: Africa’s continental split offers new insights for scientists
articles.description: Geologists say the gradual splitting of the African continent could provide unique insights into the formation of new oceans and tectonic processes.
